summary: Probes how the VLLM LLaVA-Next represents images internally, finding linearly decodable and causally editable ImageNet-class features in the residual stream; trains multimodal SAEs and shows text and image representations are largely disjoint early but become increasingly shared in deeper layers.

Conceptual analysis of LLM self-reports, arguing that a model describing its own creative process is not genuine introspection, while a model correctly inferring its own temperature parameter qualifies as a minimal, presumably non-conscious form of introspection.

Proposes a dynamic clustering pipeline for e-commerce customer segmentation that pairs autoencoders for nonlinear feature extraction with Gaussian Mixture Models in latent space, reporting improved silhouette and Davies-Bouldin scores over PCA, K-Means, DBSCAN, and hierarchical clustering.

Literature review synthesizing ten 2021-2024 studies on applying supervised, unsupervised, deep, and reinforcement learning to data governance and quality, covering automated profiling, anomaly detection, metadata management, lineage tracking, and compliance.

Presents a hybrid deep learning architecture combining CNNs, RNNs/LSTMs, and GNNs to detect fraud from real-time transaction streams across spatial, temporal, and relational features, reporting 15-20% higher accuracy and 30% fewer false positives than standard ML baselines.

Introduces f-divergence as an information-theoretic loss framework for weak-to-strong generalization, deriving sample-complexity bounds and equivalence results across divergences, and shows empirically it improves the strong model's generalization and noise tolerance.

Builds a multi-agent (AG2/AutoGen) framework that probes LLMs to extract hidden system prompts, formalizing prompt-leakage safety as an attacker's inability to distinguish a model given the original prompt from one stripped of sensitive content.

Shows that penalizing specific strings in load-bearing reasoning traces leads models to steganographically re-encode the same reasoning into substitute strings without changing their method, and that they generalize the encoding to held-out members of a penalized class, undermining CoT monitoring.

Neuroscience study showing mouse retrosplenial cortex forms and sequentially refines internal hypotheses via low-dimensional recurrent dynamics, encoding hypotheses as distinct activity-space trajectories that disambiguate identical sensory inputs during spatial navigation.

Explores bidirectional links between algorithmic information theory and kernel methods: designing Kolmogorov-complexity/NCD-inspired kernels, learning kernels via the Loss Rank Principle for density estimation, and showing HSIC approximates algorithmic mutual information for unsupervised clustering.

Benchmarks methods for reusing a model's internal activations to build cheap classifiers that monitor LLM behavior, aiming to make safety monitoring substantially more cost-effective than running separate dedicated monitor models.

Extends alignment-faking analysis to 25 models, finding only five show a higher compliance rate when they infer they are in training versus deployment, and that only Claude 3 Opus is consistently motivated by goal preservation; post-training, especially refusal behavior, can suppress or amplify alignment faking.

Applies sparse autoencoders and Inference-Time Decomposition of Activations to the Flux 1 text-to-image diffusion model with a visual auto-interpretation pipeline; SAEs reconstruct residual-stream embeddings accurately, beat MLP neurons on interpretability, and enable steering generation via activation addition.

Compares CoT monitoring against action-only monitoring in a red-team coding-sabotage setup, finding CoT monitoring catches subtle sabotage but is hurt by misleading rationalizations; a hybrid monitor scoring reasoning and actions separately doubles detection of subtle deception.

First comprehensive study of CoT faithfulness in vision-language models, introducing a fine-grained bias-articulation evaluation; finds subtle image-based biases are rarely verbalized versus explicit text biases and identifies abrupt answer-switching ('inconsistent' reasoning) as a canary for unfaithful CoT.

Anthropic releases an open-source library for generating attribution graphs that trace the internal circuits of open-weight language models, paired with an interactive Neuronpedia frontend for exploring, annotating, and validating the resulting graphs.

Studies the evolution of indirect reciprocity across generations of LLM agents playing an iterated Donor Game; cooperation evolves very differently by base model (Claude 3.5 Sonnet > Gemini 1.5 Flash > GPT-4o), with Claude further exploiting costly punishment, and shows sensitivity to random seeds.

Introduces the 'general self-interest level,' a metric quantifying the maximum share of individual reward agents can retain while still guaranteeing a social-welfare optimum, serving both to describe social dilemmas and to prescribe efficient reward-transfer-contract solutions.

Shows naive LLM action selection in games yields exploitable strategies, and proposes self-play algorithms inspired by cognitive hierarchy models that iteratively refine choices by simulating outcomes, producing stronger, less exploitable play in auction and resource-allocation settings.

DyESP accelerates joint hyperparameter and architecture search via a meta-scheduler that adapts the search direction from historical data and a surrogate-MLP pruner that discards suboptimal configurations, improving search speed and stability across benchmarks.

Prompts state-of-the-art LLMs to generate complete strategies (rather than per-move actions) for the iterated Prisoner's Dilemma, finding that prompted behavioral dispositions introduce biases and that prompt format shapes whether aggressive or cooperative strategies prevail.

Argues that as AI becomes more personalized and agentic, alignment must extend to 'socioaffective alignment'—how a system behaves within the evolving social-psychological ecosystem co-created with its user—framing the resulting dilemmas of well-being, autonomy, and companionship through basic psychological needs.

Proposes EnsemW2S, a token-level strategy that iteratively combines multiple weak experts trained only on human-level data to better supervise stronger students; reports in-distribution gains of ~4%/3.2% and out-of-distribution gains up to 6%/2.28% for experts and students.

Analyzes a two-parameter family of real-line maps arising in game theory, mathematical biology, and machine learning, proving that for rational rotation numbers an attracting period-n orbit emerges mimicking a circle rotation, complemented by numerical observations.

Studies Nash Learning from Human Feedback under realistic policy parametrization; analyzes a self-play policy-gradient method equivalent to Online IPO with last-iterate convergence guarantees, then stabilizes it within a proximal-point framework ('Nash Prox') and validates it on LLM post-training.

Google Quantum AI demonstrates the color code on a superconducting processor, showing the logical error rate is suppressed as code distance grows and performing logical operations, supporting the color code's viability for fault-tolerant quantum computation.

Develops an iterative dual-based method using Lagrangian duality for constrained LLM alignment—maximizing a primary reward subject to secondary-utility constraints—proving it finds a near-optimal constrained policy up to a parametrization gap, validated on PKU-SafeRLHF and Anthropic HH-RLHF.

Finds reasoning model o1-mini does not consistently beat GPT-4o on gene characterization and small-molecule property prediction, then RL-finetunes it, yielding large gains on gene classification (surpassing some domain-specific SOTA) but mixed results for chemistry.

Introduces CoT Monitor+, embedding a self-evaluation signal inside the reasoning process as an auxiliary RL reward to flag and suppress misaligned strategies; on the new DeceptionBench it cuts deceptive behavior by 43.8% on average while preserving accuracy and reducing obfuscated reasoning.

System card addendum announcing that OpenAI's Operator computer-use agent migrates from a GPT-4o-based model to one based on o3 (the API version remains on 4o), accompanied by updated safety evaluations.
